items.prompt: The inverse of a function f exists if and only if f is:
For a bijective f : X -> Y with inverse g : Y -> X, the domain of g equals:

options: the whole set Y minus the range of f

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch02 Sec 2.2 Basic concepts

items.prompt: Why does an inverse trigonometric function have several branches?
items.answer: the trig function is bijective on several different intervals

expected.correct: the trig function is bijective on several different intervals
options: the trig function is bijective on several different intervals
the trig function has several different formulas
the trig function is not periodic

items.prompt: Find the principal value of sin-inverse(1/sqrt2). Give your answer as a multiple of pi: enter the numerical coefficient when the angle is written as (coefficient) x pi. (Hint: pi/4 has coefficient 0.25.)
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch02 Example 1 (principal value of sin-inverse(1/sqrt2) = pi/4)

items.prompt: Which statement is correct about the notation sin-inverse x?
items.answer: sin-inverse x is an angle, not 1/sin x

expected.correct: sin-inverse x is an angle, not 1/sin x
options: sin-inverse x is an angle, not 1/sin x
sin-inverse x is undefined for all x

items.prompt: The value of an inverse trigonometric function that lies in the range of its principal branch is called its:
For which values of x is the identity sin-inverse(sin x) = x valid?
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch02 Sec 2.3 cancellation identity

items.prompt: Find sin-inverse(sin(3pi/5)). Write the answer as (coefficient) x pi and enter the coefficient. (Hint: reduce 3pi/5 into the principal range first.)
Evaluate the principal value of cot-inverse(-1/sqrt3), explaining which principal range you use and how you choose the angle.

expected.rubric: Let cot-inverse(-1/sqrt3) = y, so cot y = -1/sqrt3. The principal value branch of cot-inverse has range (0, pi). Since cot(2pi/3) = -1/sqrt3 and 2pi/3 lies in (0, pi), the principal value is 2pi/3.
markPoints: Set cot y = -1/sqrt3 and use the principal range (0, pi)

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch02 Example 2 (principal value of cot-inverse(-1/sqrt3) = 2pi/3)
